theorem IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := by
    have h₁ : HasLaw G (G(V, p)) P := hG.hasLaw
    have h₂ : HasLaw H (G(V, q)) P := hY.hasLaw
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) (G(V, p) ⊓ G(V, q)) P := by
      refine HasLaw.comp _ h₁ h₂
      <;>
      (try simp_all [HasLaw]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [radonNikodymComposite]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Measure.map_inf_independent_left]) <;>
      (try
        {
          apply And.intro
          <;>
          (try apply IsProbabilityMeasure.isFinite) <;>
          (try simp_all [IsProbabilityMeasure]) <;>
          (try apply MeasurableSpace.isFinite) <;>
          (try simp_all [IsProbabilityMeasure, MeasurableSpace.isFinite])
        }
      ) <;>
      (try
        {
          apply And.intro
          <;>
          (try apply IsProbabilityMeasure.totientIsSubCosMeasure) <;>
          (try simp_all [IsProbabilityMeasure]) <;>
          (try apply MeasurableSpace.isFinite) <;>
          (try simp_all [IsProbabilityMeasure, MeasurableSpace.isFinite])
        }
      )
    have h₄ : G(V, p) ⊓ G(V, q) = G(V, p * q) := by
      classical
      simp_all [G, binomialRandom, MeasureTheory.Independent, BinaryInf]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [isBernoulliOn_iff]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Ber]) <;>
      (try simp_all [infinitePi]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Finset.prod_mul_distrib]) <;>
      (try simp_all [pi_prod]) <;>
      (try simp_all [mul_comm]) <;>
      (try simp_all [mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try simp_all [mul_left_comm]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Finset.prod_const]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Finset.card_univ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [pow_mul]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Set.prod_inter_distrib_left]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Set.prod_inter_distrib_right])
      <;>
      (try
        {
          ext1 s
          simp_all [Set.mem_inter_iff]
          <;>
          (try
            {
              constructor <;> intro h <;>
              (try simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]) <;>
              (try aesop)
            }
          )
        }
      )
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  aemeasurable := by
    simp_all [IsBinomialRandom, HasLaw]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [aemeasurable]) <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₁ : (fun (ω : Ω) => G ω ⊓ H ω : Ω → SimpleGraph V) = (fun (ω : Ω) => G ω ⊓ H ω : Ω → SimpleGraph V) := rfl
        rw [h₁]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [aemeasurable]) <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₂ : (fun (ω : Ω) => G ω ⊓ H ω : Ω → SimpleGraph V) = (fun (ω : Ω) => G ω ⊓ H ω : Ω → SimpleGraph V) := rfl
            rw [h₂]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [aemeasurable]) <;>
            (try
              {
                apply aemeasurable_inf (h₁ : Measurable fun ω : Ω => G ω) (h₂ : Measurable fun ω : Ω => H ω)
              }
            )
          }
        )
      }
    )

          -- Use the fact that G is binomial random, hence measurable
          have h₄ : Measurable G := by
            sorry
          have h₅ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω).adj u v) := by

      have h₃ : Measurable H := by
        rw [SimpleGraph.measurable_iff_adj]

          have h₆ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω => (H ω).adj u v) := by
            -- Extract the measurability of the adjacency map from the measurability of H

          exact h₆
        exact h₄
      -- Prove that the infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
